name: Vincent Price's Kedgeree
recipeIngredient: 1-lb can of salmon or enough poached fresh salmon (1-1/2 lbs) to measure 2 cups
1 can chicken consomme plus enough water to measure 2 cups
recipeInstructions:
1. Flake the salmon into pieces and set aside.
2. Preheat oven to moderate (350°F).
3. In a saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 5 minutes until softened.
4. Add salt, 1 cup of rice, and the chicken consomme plus enough water to measure 2 cups to the saucepan. Stir to combine.
5. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer, covered tightly, for 30 minutes without stirring.
6. Remove the lid and stir the rice gently. Let it steam uncovered for an additional 5 minutes.
7. In a large bowl, mix 1 cup of the cooked rice pilaf with the flaked salmon. Add the diced hard-cooked egg, bechamel sauce, and salt to taste. Mix well.
8. Place the mixture in a greased glass baking dish measuring approximately 8 inches round and 2 inches deep. Create a ring with the mixture in the center of the dish.
9. Fill the center of the dish with the remaining flaked salmon and cover completely with the leftover rice.
10. Dot the top with the remaining tablespoon of butter.
11. Place the baking dish in a pan containing about an inch of hot water. Cover the dish with greased brown paper.
12. Bake in the moderate oven (350°F) for 20 minutes.
Serve with your favorite curry sauce.
